seo.title: VEED Alternative for Lyric Videos — Song-First AI, Free
seo.description: Need a VEED alternative for lyric videos? Start from audio, auto-sync lyrics, generate song-first visuals, and export without timeline editing.

hero.badge: VEED alternative — AI lyric video maker, free
hero.subhead: VEED is a general video editor — great for subtitles and talking heads, but lyric videos still mean manual text placement and timeline work. LyricVideoMaker starts from the song: upload audio, AI auto-transcribes lyrics, syncs every line to the beat, and generates visuals. Export free, no subscription needed.

trust: Export free, no subscription or credit card

comparisonTable.title: VEED vs LyricVideoMaker for Lyric Videos
comparisonTable.description: VEED is useful when you need a general editor. LyricVideoMaker is faster when the job is turning a song into a lyric video.

rows.label: Starting point

comparisonTable.note: The difference is workflow shape: VEED gives you editing tools; LyricVideoMaker turns the song itself into the project.

howItWorks.title: How This VEED Alternative Makes Lyric Videos

steps.title: Upload your song
steps.description: Drop an MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, or OGG file — or paste a link. No account needed to start, and no manual lyric typing required. Unlike VEED, there's no blank timeline — the song drives the workflow from the start.

steps.title: AI transcribes and syncs
steps.description: AI transcribes lyrics from the audio automatically and syncs every line to the beat — in 50+ languages. Click any line to edit. No dragging text boxes, no adjusting timestamps frame by frame. AI also generates visuals matched to the song's mood.

steps.title: Preview and export
steps.description: Watch the full lyric video before publishing. Confirm lyrics are accurate and timing feels right, then export in 16:9 for YouTube, 9:16 for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts, or 1:1 for feed posts. Free to export, no subscription required.

whyChoose.title: Why Make Lyric Videos on LyricVideoMaker Instead of a Timeline Editor
whyChoose.description: VEED is a capable general editor, but making a lyric video in VEED means typing lyrics, placing text, syncing timestamps, and building on a timeline. LyricVideoMaker eliminates all of that — upload a song and get a finished lyric video.

highlight.title: Song in, lyric video out — no timeline
highlight.description: VEED gives you a timeline and text tools. LyricVideoMaker gives you a finished lyric video from a song upload — lyrics transcribed, synced, and visualized automatically.

cards.title: Song-first project setup
cards.description: The upload starts with audio analysis, lyrics, timing, and visuals instead of asking you to assemble a blank timeline.

cards.title: Review instead of reconstruct
cards.description: You review synced lyric lines and visual direction instead of recreating the song section by section with text boxes.

cards.title: Release assets stay aligned
cards.description: Full-song videos and short clips can share the same lyric timing and visual direction, so release content feels consistent.

items.question: Is LyricVideoMaker free as a VEED alternative?
items.answer: Yes. As a VEED alternative, LyricVideoMaker is free — free credits to start, export included, no subscription or credit card.

items.question: How is LyricVideoMaker different from VEED for lyric videos?
items.answer: VEED is a general video editor — you type lyrics, place text, and sync timestamps on a timeline. LyricVideoMaker auto-transcribes lyrics from audio, syncs to the beat, generates visuals, and gives you a finished lyric video. No timeline needed.

items.question: Do I need to type lyrics like in VEED?
items.answer: No. Instead of typing and placing text like in VEED, AI transcribes the lyrics from your audio in 50+ languages. You just review and edit.

items.question: Is VEED still better for some projects?
items.answer: Yes — VEED is better for general video editing, talking-head content, subtitled clips, and mixed social content. For lyric videos specifically, LyricVideoMaker is faster because it's built for that one job.

items.question: Can I use exported lyric videos commercially?
items.answer: Yes. Your exports are yours for monetized channels and paid releases — the same freedom you'd expect from VEED. Just make sure you have the rights to the music you upload.

items.question: How hard is it to switch from VEED?
items.answer: There's nothing to migrate. Upload the same song you used in VEED and you'll have a finished lyric video in a couple of minutes — no project files to move, no setup.
